query: SELECT session_id, target_url, lab_status, phase, progress, SUBSTRING(llm_analysis_json, 1, 3000) as llm_snippet FROM training_lab_sessions WHERE session_id = 'lab-1772928125108-a84a74ae' LIMIT 1
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2k2SuDvpDb6F44q.5f28f8362a85 --database VmWWcXQYZJYuALRdNNvsC2 --execute SELECT session_id, target_url, lab_status, phase, progress, SUBSTRING(llm_analysis_json, 1, 3000) as llm_snippet FROM training_lab_sessions WHERE session_id = 'lab-1772928125108-a84a74ae' LIMIT 1

rows.llm_snippet: {"attackChains": [{}, {}, {}, {}], "executiveSummary": "The automated scan results provided zero findings, however, this is largely due to misconfiguration of tools and the inherent limitations of automated scanning against a known vulnerable application. Manual analysis and targeted testing are crucial to identify the numerous security flaws present in this training environment.", "findings": [{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}], "missedAreas": ["Comprehensive SQL Injection testing on all input fields and parameters.", "Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) testing on all input fields, URL parameters, and reflected/stored contexts.", "Manual review of authentication and session management mechanisms for weaknesses (e.g., weak password policies, session fixation, insecure session IDs).", "Thorough testing of access control mechanisms, including horizontal and vertical privilege escalation attempts.", "File upload functionality testing for arbitrary file upload and execution vulnerabilities.", "Business logic flaws, including parameter tampering, race conditions, and improper handling of application-specific workflows.", "API endpoint enumeration and testing for vulnerabilities like broken object level authorization (BOLA) and excessive data exposure.", "Client-side security testing (e.g., DOM XSS, insecure local storage).", "Detailed analysis of server-side configuration for misconfigurations (e.g., directory listings, default credentials, exposed administrative interfaces).", "Testing for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities.", "Manual enumeration of hidden directories and files beyond common wordlists, potentially using application-specific knowledge.", "Analysis of error messages for information disclosure.", "Password reset functionality testing for logic flaws or token bypasses.", "In-depth analysis of HTTP headers for security best practices (e.g., HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options).", "Source code review (if available) for deeper insights into vulnerabilities."], "recommendations": ["Implement robust input validation and sanitization on all user-supplied data to prevent injection attacks (SQLi, XSS, Command Injection).", "Adopt a strong authentication and session management policy, including multi-factor authentication, secure password hashing, and proper session invalidation.", "Enforce strict access control checks at every layer of the application, ensuring users can only access resources and perform actions for which they are authorized.", "Implement secure file upload mechanisms, including content type validation, size limits, and storing files outside the web root with unique, unguessable names.", "Regularly update and patch all software components, including the operating system, web server, database, and application frameworks, to mitigate known vulnerabilities.", "Implement comprehensive error handling that avoids disclosing sensitive information to users.", "Configure web servers and application servers securely, disabling
